ops.text: cc-present 0.10.0: a pack block whose interaction schema is note-only (a comment affordance, `{note}` and nothing else) still counts as a full decision unit — the getaway booking sheet with `presentation: "board"` renders a "0/2 decided" submit bar and jump-dots over what is semantically pure content. Nothing is gated, so it's cosmetic, but every content board that adds comment support inherits decision chrome. A per-block opt-out in the pack manifest (e.g. `decision = false` on a block table, or treating an interaction schema whose only property is `note` as non-decisional) would let packs add comments without decision semantics. Observed 2026-07-18 during getaway pack v2 QA; getaway docs now paper over it by defining Submit on that round as the booked-it confirmation.
